summary:
• Storm Response: Recent severe storms have caused extensive damage, including over 260 downed power lines, necessitating immediate action from emergency services and utility companies.
  • Community Engagement: The City is promoting family-friendly events, such as the "Stars, Stripes & Splashes" pool party, to enhance community involvement and recreational opportunities.
  • Public Trust Initiative: The OECD is launching a survey to assess public trust in institutions, providing an opportunity for Chattanooga to engage in broader discussions on governance and community trust.

items.title: Join the conversation on trust in public institutions.   Hear what people across 38 countries had to say as we launch th...
items.content_text:
Join the conversation on trust in public institutions.  
 Hear what people across 38 countries had to say as we launch the OECD Survey on Drivers of Trust in Public Institutions 2026 Results. 
 This report provides new evidence on how trust in public institutions has evolved since 2021. It also examines the implications for improving public governance and delivering better outcomes for citizens. 
 The event will feature opening remarks by OECD Secretary-General Mathias Cormann with a presentation of the results of the survey by Director for Public Governance at the OECD,  Elsa Pilichowski.
 This event will offer valuable insights for policymakers, practitioners and researchers working on public governance and institutional reform. 
 Please register to attend: https://brnw.ch/21x3zn3  
 #OECDTrustSurvey

The event will feature opening remarks by OECD Secretary-General Mathias Cormann with a presentation of the results of the survey by Director for Public Governance at the OECD, Elsa Pilichowski.
This event will offer valuable insights for policymakers, practitioners and researchers working on public governance and institutional reform.

items.title: A championship coach. A mentor. A Moc legend. 🐐 After 25 seasons leading Chattanooga Mocs Softball, Coach Frank Reed ha...
items.content_text:
A championship coach. A mentor. A Moc legend. 🐐 After 25 seasons leading Chattanooga Mocs Softball, Coach Frank Reed has announced his retirement.
 His record speaks for itself: 824 wins, 10 Southern Conference regular-season titles, 11 tournament championships, and 11 NCAA Tournament appearances. But his greatest impact can be found in the thousands of student-athletes, coaches, and families whose lives he helped shape along the way.
 Thank you, Coach Reed, for 25 remarkable years of service to UTC. Your legacy of excellence, leadership, and care for student-athletes will continue to inspire future generations of Mocs.
 Read more on GoMocs.com: https://gomocs.com/…/softball-reed-retires-with-a-champions…

items.title: 🔧 Storm Update: We're Working for You A powerful storm with high winds has caused widespread damage across the Chattano...
items.content_text:
🔧 Storm Update: We're Working for You
 A powerful storm with high winds has caused widespread damage across the Chattanooga area — including more than 260 downed power lines, broken poles, and other damage. Our crews mobilized immediately, and we're not stopping until every customer is back on.
 Here's where things stand:
 ⚡ 11,700 customers avoided a lasting outage thanks to Chattanooga's automated smart grid, which prevented or auto-restored their power.
 👷 EPB is in the process of deploying 400+ utility workers, including additional crews we've called in from neighboring communities to help speed things up.
 🏠 We expect to restore thousands of customers by morning, but with damage this severe full restoration will require continuous effort through tomorrow.
 To our customers still waiting: thank you for your patience. Our teams are working around the clock — through the night and into tomorrow — to get your lights back on as quickly and safely as possible. 💙
 Stay safe, steer clear of downed lines, and report outages using the free MyEPB app.

Here's where things stand:
⚡ 11,700 customers avoided a lasting outage thanks to Chattanooga's automated smart grid, which prevented or auto-restored their power.
👷 EPB is in the process of deploying 400+ utility workers, including additional crews we've called in from neighboring communities to help speed things up.
🏠 We expect to restore thousands of customers by morning, but with damage this severe full restoration will require continuous effort through tomorrow.
To our customers still waiting: thank you for your patience. Our teams are working around the clock — through the night and into tomorrow — to get your lights back on as quickly and safely as possible. 💙
Stay safe, steer clear of downed lines, and report outages using the free MyEPB app.

items.title: Please share to help spread the word!A #TNSilverAlert has been issued on behalf of the Chattanooga Police Department for...
items.content_text:
Please share to help spread the word!
A #TNSilverAlert has been issued on behalf of the Chattanooga Police Department for 83-year-old Betty Miller. 
 Betty was last seen on Monday leaving her home in Chattanooga and is believed to be traveling in a 2008 Red Chevy Cobalt with TN tag PD69088.
 Betty is 5’4” tall, 165 lbs., with gray hair and brown eyes. She has a medical condition that may impair her ability to return safely without assistance.
  If you have seen Betty please call the Chattanooga Police Department at 423-698-2525 or TBI at 1-800-TBI-FIND.

items.content_text:
Upcoming Events Post
 🌞🎉 Summer fun is in full swing at Hamilton County Parks! From patriotic celebrations and colorful tie-dye creations to movie nights under the stars and action-packed field day fun, something exciting is happening all month long. Grab your lawn chairs, blankets, friends, and family — and come make unforgettable summer memories with us! 🍿🎨🌳

 🗓️ Tuesday, June 30th, 2:00 pm – 9:00 pm

 Movie Night: National Treasure at Riverpark
 🗓️ Saturday, July 2nd, 8:00 pm - 11:30 pm
 📍 Riverpark Hubert Fry Center Oval 4301 Amnicola Highway, Chattanooga, TN 37406
 Movie Night: The Incredibles at The Harrison Center
 🗓️ Friday, July 24th, 5:00 pm – 8:00 pm
 📍Harrison Center Auditorium 5637 TN-58, Harrison, TN 37341

items.title: Chattanooga was blasted by storms Monday night (6/22/26), making things very busy for Red Shift firefighters across the...
items.content_text:
Chattanooga was blasted by storms Monday night (6/22/26), making things very busy for Red Shift firefighters across the city. Calls quickly poured into 911, like wires down, fires, flooding, and large trees on homes. We have no injuries to report at this time, thankfully. The structural damage caused by downed trees displaced several families. Trees fell in multiple locations, including: 
 4100 block of Kirkland Avenue
 300 block of S Howell Avenue
 800 block of Mount Vernon Circle 
 2000 block of N Chamberlain Avenue
 3300 block of Adkins Road 
 Please do not drive through flooded roadways or drive over downed power lines.

items.title: Join the City of Chattanooga Department of Community Development Aquatics Division for a fun, family-friendly evening at...
items.content_text:
Join the City of Chattanooga Department of Community Development Aquatics Division for a fun, family-friendly evening at Stars, Stripes & Splashes! — The Great American Pool Party!
 Enjoy open swim, splash pad access, live DJ entertainment, patriotic games, a photo/selfie station, and complimentary light refreshments as we celebrate summer in red, white, and blue.
 ⏰📆Thursday, July 2, 2026, from 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM!
 📍Warner Park Pool on Thursday, July 2, 2026, from 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM!
 Admission is $2.50 and everyone is welcome!
 Get your tickets today by visiting https://cha.city/250 or by texting “250” to 423-643-7300. 
 Come ready to swim, splash, and celebrate!

items.title: No, we’re not building an underground water park. 😎 Today we got a look inside the tanks being built along I-75 near ou...
items.content_text:
No, we’re not building an underground water park. 😎
 Today we got a look inside the tanks being built along I-75 near our Brown Acres Golf Course  These giant tanks along I-75 are actually part of a major project that will store up to 30 million gallons of stormwater during heavy rains. Think of them as Chattanooga’s version of a giant “pause button” for excess water and help clean. 
 They may look like something out of a sci-fi movie now, but by 2027 they’ll help protect our water system and keep local waterways cleaner like the South Chickamauga Creek.
 Now, this project will not be complete till fall of 2027, which means the greenway from Camp Jordan to Brained Levee will remain closed till then. 
 Also, if anyone asks, yes… we checked. Echoes are incredible in there. 🎤😂l

They may look like something out of a sci-fi movie now, but by 2027 they’ll help protect our water system and keep local waterways cleaner like the South Chickamauga Creek.

items.title: Brainerd Hills, your new pavilion at Benham-Williams Park is officially open!  Huge kudos to everyone who worked to pull...
items.content_text:
Brainerd Hills, your new pavilion at Benham-Williams Park is officially open! 
 Huge kudos to everyone who worked to pull this off! This is the first "in-house" pavilion completely designed and constructed by our world-class team. Thank you to District 6 Councilwoman Jenni Berz and the Brainerd Hills Neighborhood Association for rallying the neighborhood for a rainy-day ribbon cutting and cookout in the park! It was a great chance to put this structure to the test, and it passed with flying colors. We are looking forward to opening more great structures like this in parks across our beautiful city.

items.title: In the modern world, where digital information is always at risk, security is more important than ever. UTC Research Ass...
items.content_text:
In the modern world, where digital information is always at risk, security is more important than ever.
 UTC Research Assistant Professor Eric Hoang H. Nguyen has always been interested in protecting information.
 “It’s a little bit of my habit,” Nguyen said. “I want to learn about a system, find problems with the system and try to solve the problems. That’s why I chose the topic I did, as it’s most relevant to security.
 🔗  Read more here: https://blog.utc.edu/…/behind-the-research-at-utc-dr-eric-…/

items.title: Earlier today, around 9:30 A.M., HCSO deputies responded to reports of a suspicious vehicle in the vicinity of Hixson Pi...
items.content_text:
Earlier today, around 9:30 A.M., HCSO deputies responded to reports of a suspicious vehicle in the vicinity of Hixson Pike and Thrasher Pike. After locating the vehicle, deputies made contact with the driver, who then fled, initiating a pursuit. 
 The pursuit ended at the Redoubt Soccer Complex, where deputies apprehended the suspect and took him into custody, transporting him to the Hamilton County Jail and Detention Center.
 Antonio Pickett was found to have active warrants and is now facing additional charges:
 Active Warrants:
 -  Reckless Endangerment
 -  Theft of Property
 -  Auto Burglary
 -  Evading Arrest
 New Charges:
 -  Evading Arrest
 -  Resisting Stop, Frisk, Halt, and Arrest
 -  Reckless Driving
 -  Driving on a Revoked Driver’s License 
 -  Vandalism 
 -  Possession of a Controlled Substance for Distribution (x2)
 -  Possession of Drug Paraphernalia 
 Additional traffic charges are pending.
 This information is preliminary and subject to change. No further details are available.
items.content_html:

items.title: City of East Ridge Receives 2026 Community Service Award for Tourism Attraction! 🏆 Last week, the City of East Ridge wa...
items.content_text:
City of East Ridge Receives 2026 Community Service Award for Tourism Attraction! 🏆
 Last week, the City of East Ridge was honored to have been recognized by the Greater Chattanooga Hospitality Association- GCHA with the 2026 Community Service Award for Tourism Attraction in recognition of the East Ridge Parks and Recreation Department. 🙌🏼 
 While Camp Jordan serves as a major destination for sports tourism, outdoor recreation, festivals, and special events, this recognition reflects the broader impact of the East Ridge Parks & Recreation Department and the many ways it serves both residents and visitors throughout the entire community in #EastRidgeTN. 🤍
 From hosting regional tournaments and community events at Camp Jordan Park, to providing year-round programs and services through the East Ridge Community Center, and welcoming guests at the recently opened Venue 1921 at East Ridge, our Parks & Recreation Department continues to enhance quality of life while supporting tourism, economic activity, and community engagement here in our own community every single day! 
 This award would not be possible without the dedication of our Parks & Recreation staff, the support of Mayor Brian Williams and City Councilmembers, the collaboration of City departments, and the many community partners, volunteers, coaches, organizations, and residents who help make these facilities and programs successful in every way possible.
 As we continue investing in our parks, recreation facilities, community programming, and public spaces, we remain committed to creating opportunities that bring people together and showcase all that East Ridge has to offer! 
 Please join us in congratulating the East Ridge Parks & Recreation Department on this outstanding achievement! 👏🏼
 Let’s keep moving forward, East Ridge!

Let’s keep moving forward, East Ridge!

CAMP COLLEGE 2026 PHOTO RECAP!  Camp College gives students the chance to experience college life, ask questions, meet n...
items.content_text:
CAMP COLLEGE 2026 PHOTO RECAP! 
 Camp College gives students the chance to experience college life, ask questions, meet new people, and start thinking about what comes next after high school.
 It’s one of the ways PEF helps launch students into their next step with more confidence, support, and possibility. We had such a great time this year with our students at Sewanee. See you next year! 
 #CampCollege #PublicEducationForward #PEFStudents #CollegeAccess #StudentSuccess

Camp College gives students the chance to experience college life, ask questions, meet new people, and start thinking about what comes next after high school.
It’s one of the ways PEF helps launch students into their next step with more confidence, support, and possibility. We had such a great time this year with our students at Sewanee. See you next year!
#CampCollege #PublicEducationForward #PEFStudents #CollegeAccess #StudentSuccess

items.title: Let's send another shout out to civil engineer and project manager, Elizabeth Goss, (holding paper) for letting us tag a...
items.content_text:
Let's send another shout out to civil engineer and project manager, Elizabeth Goss, (holding paper) for letting us tag along and visit the equalization tanks near Brown Acres Golf course.  Both tanks are 240 feet in diameter and provide storage capacity of up to 15 million gallons each of excess wastewater during significant rain events.  This structure diverts excess wastewater, not flood water,  from existing buried sewer system pipeline when excess wastewater otherwise overflows into local waterways.  
 #CleanWaterMatters #waterquality #cleanwater #PublicWorks #engineers #civilengineering #womeninpublicworks #womenengineersrock
items.content_html:

items.title: Summer in Chattanooga is best spent outside. We were named the country’s first National Park City for a reason! From kay...
items.content_text:
Summer in Chattanooga is best spent outside. We were named the country’s first National Park City for a reason!
 From kayaking the Tennessee River and hiking Lookout or Signal Mountain to biking and exploring the River Walk, there are endless ways to enjoy the season outdoors.
 Check out upcoming adventures with Outdoor Chattanooga for ways to get involved and make the most of summer in the city.

items.title: Just moments ago, Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office deputies responded to what turned out to be a swatting call from a re...
items.content_text:
Just moments ago, Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office deputies responded to what turned out to be a swatting call from a residence near Standifer Gap and Maplewood Drive. HCSO personnel will always take any threat made seriously until determined to be false or unfounded.
 There is no threat to the public and the incident is under investigation.
 This information is preliminary and subject to change. No further details are available.

items.title: Friendly reminder for those interested in our last Esophageal Cancer Screenings coming up this Wednesday, June 24, 2026...
items.content_text:
Friendly reminder for those interested in our last Esophageal Cancer Screenings coming up this Wednesday, June 24, 2026 from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. WE STILL HAVE SLOTS AVAILABLE!
 The Chattanooga Fire and Police Pension Fund is allowing Lucid Diagnostics, a medical diagnostics company, to provide Esoguard screenings during June of this year to aide in early detection of esophageal precancer to active and retired members at our Fund office (6009 John Douglass Drive, Chattanooga, TN 37421).
 💲COST:
 *No out of pocket cost to the member/spouse (screening is billed through insurance) . You are not responsible for any portion not covered by insurance, up to and including the total cost of the test if your insurance denies coverage. A “What to Expect After EsoGuard” document that provides will be provided to you at the screening event.
 Information has been emailed to retired participants regarding scheduling. If you are interested in scheduling a screening and did not receive an email, please contact our office asap.

items.title: Last week, SETD staff came together for our Employee Appreciation and Staff Development Day at Spare Time, celebrating the people...
items.content_text:
Last week, SETD staff came together for our Employee Appreciation and Staff Development Day at Spare Time, celebrating the people who make our mission possible every day.

A special congratulations to five team members recognized for their years of dedicated service to the organization, ranging from 5 to 25 years with SETD. Their commitment, expertise, and passion have helped strengthen our organization and the communities we serve across Southeast Tennessee.

Thank you for your years of service and for the lasting impact you continue to make. Here’s to celebrating milestones, building connections, and recognizing the incredible people behind the work.

Their commitment, expertise, and passion have helped strengthen our organization and the communities we serve across Southeast Tennessee. Thank you for your years of service and for the lasting impact you continue to make. Here’s to celebrating milestones, building connections, and recognizing the incredible people behind the work.

items.title: Build Your Business Quick Tip: Host tastings or demonstrations to showcase products and services  Want more people to un...
items.content_text:
Build Your Business Quick Tip: Host tastings or demonstrations to showcase products and services 
 Want more people to understand what makes your business special? Show them. Hosting product demonstrations or tastings gives potential customers a chance to experience your products and services firsthand. These experiences help build trust, create meaningful connections, and leave a lasting impression that can turn curiosity into loyalty. 
 Featured: Pedestrian Wine and Cheese Shop

items.title: CHAPublicWorks Posted
Our new Liberty Bell replica is now on display in Boy Scout Park thanks to Signal Mountain Public Works! Produced by Sis...
items.content_text: Our new Liberty Bell replica is now on display in Boy Scout Park thanks to Signal Mountain Public Works! Produced by Sisken Steel and donated by Hamilton County and the DAR, it's a perfect photo stop as we gear up for the parade on July 4th and America's 250th. Special thanks to County Commissioner Chip Baker for helping bring this patriotic addition to Signal Mountain. Be sure to check it out!

items.title: Want to Make and Sip? Member artists of Southeast Chapter of TN are getting together to chill and create all of own art...
items.content_text: Want to Make and Sip? Member artists of Southeast Chapter of TN are getting together to chill and create all of own art forms at the East Lake Pavilion. Want to watch? All are welcome to observe as masters of crafts and fine art create, as different art forms: painting, ceramics, bone sculpting, weaving and much more can be observed. #chattanooga #fineart #finecraft #artist #chattanoogafun

items.title: As summer heats up, there’s no shade sheltering unhoused community members seeking lunch from the Chatt Foundation. Here...
items.content_text:
As summer heats up, there’s no shade sheltering unhoused community members seeking lunch from the Chatt Foundation. Here’s how a long-anticipated sidewalk repair left the formerly tree-lined sidewalk exposed. 
 “Just very poor timing,” City Forester Cole Johnson told us.
 For more details on why the trees had to go and why the cutting happened just before summer, go to chattamatters.com.

items.title: It is with great sadness that Sheriff Austin Garrett and the men and women of the Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office annou...
items.content_text:
It is with great sadness that Sheriff Austin Garrett and the men and women of the Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office announce the death of retired Detective Blake Daniel.
 Detective Daniel retired from the HCSO in 2014 after working for the agency for 26 years.
 Please join us in keeping Daniel's family, friends, and former colleagues in your thoughts and prayers during this difficult time.
items.content_html:
It is with great sadness that Sheriff Austin Garrett and the men and women of the Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office announce the death of retired Detective Blake Daniel.
Detective Daniel retired from the HCSO in 2014 after working for the agency for 26 years.
Please join us in keeping Daniel's family, friends, and former colleagues in your thoughts and prayers during this difficult time.

items.title: Safety is the foundation of a better corridor—through safer crossings, better lighting, and more predictable travel for...
items.content_text:
Safety is the foundation of a better corridor—through safer crossings, better lighting, and more predictable travel for everyone.
 Share your safety concerns and ideas at the Community Open House on June 24.
 Route 4 will be fare-free from 3-9 PM and shuttle service will be provided from Barger Academy to the community center from 4-8 PM.
 To learn more, visit www.connectthecorrdior.com

items.title: Young and older people in Finland report higher levels of loneliness than their peers in other #OECD countries. Bringing...
items.content_text:
Young and older people in Finland report higher levels of loneliness than their peers in other #OECD countries. Bringing people closer together could improve mental and physical well-being, strengthen community resilience and contribute to healthy ageing.
 Our latest analysis, supported by @[urn:li:organization:657962:Sosiaali- ja terveysministeriö - Ministry of Social Affairs and Health, FI], examines labour market outcomes, skills, health, subjective well-being and social connections at different stages of life in Finland. It identifies the groups that may need the most support and the policy areas that could help improve well-being for everyone. 
 Discover more insights from the report: https://brnw.ch/21x3y1B
items.content_html:
Young and older people in Finland report higher levels of loneliness than their peers in other #OECD countries. Bringing people closer together could improve mental and physical well-being, strengthen community resilience and contribute to healthy ageing.
Our latest analysis, supported by @[urn:li:organization:657962:Sosiaali- ja terveysministeriö - Ministry of Social Affairs and Health, FI], examines labour market outcomes, skills, health, subjective well-being and social connections at different stages of life in Finland. It identifies the groups that may need the most support and the policy areas that could help improve well-being for everyone.
Discover more insights from the report: https://brnw.ch/21x3y1B

items.title: 🔍 Join us for Tech a Look: Claude 2.0! Enjoyed our first workshop on Claude—or missed it and want to learn more? This one-hour ses...
items.content_text:
🔍 Join us for Tech a Look: Claude 2.0!

Enjoyed our first workshop on Claude—or missed it and want to learn more? This one-hour session will take a deeper dive into how Claude can help you save time, improve communication, and work more efficiently.

Through demonstrations and guided activities, you'll learn practical techniques for using AI in document creation, project development, and everyday work. Whether you're in business, education, nonprofit leadership, or simply looking to boost productivity, you'll leave with ideas you can put to use immediately.

📅 Wednesday, June 24
⏰ 4:30 PM

🎟️ Register today: https://buff.ly/cJM418z 

#ChaTech #TechALook #ClaudeAI #ArtificialIntelligence #Productivity
